headline: CCJ oversold. CCJ and PWR cooling below MA50. Entry window open.
marketContext: Cycle is in Expansion / risk-on (confidence 87%) — posture is defensive — protect capital first. Fundamentals are running ahead of price in Gold & Silver — Correction Within a Structural Bull and AI Infrastructure Hardware — Rate Compression, Not Demand Break — the market's fear is the opportunity. Fed Hike Repricing — The Q3 Macro Axis, NAVN — Profitability Inflection, Parabolic Price is priced ahead of fundamentals — don't chase.
groupAContext: BWXT (RSI 41.4, -23.1% from peak), PLTR (RSI 48.1, -38.9% from peak), APP (RSI 49.2, -32% from peak) are cooling — early-entry thesis intact. KNSL is extended (RSI 59.5) — no new entry here, let momentum resolve. Best setup: BWXT at RSI 41.4 — -23.1% from 52-week high with narrative not yet priced by institutions.
groupBContext: CCJ (RSI 38.1) is technically oversold — historically a high-quality entry signal for moat names. PWR (RSI 41.9, -8% vs MA50), NXT (RSI 43.5, -11% vs MA50), GOOGL (RSI 47, -4% vs MA50), CLS (RSI 48.6, -5% vs MA50) are below MA50 with cooling RSI — approaching entry range. Highest conviction: CLS (score 74) — use RSI and MA50 levels to time entry, not to validate the thesis.

frameworkPanel.thesis: Plateau rates ~3-3.5%, no zero-rate rescue, adjustment risk elevated (Jun)
frameworkPanel.activation: Activation trigger: Baa-10Y credit spread widening past ~2.5 while Fed holds (now 1.56)
frameworkPanel.calibration_finding: Measured across 377 names (6m forward): deep dislocation (>35% below high) won only 35.7% of the time; names within 10% of highs won 58% (median 5.6%). Dislocation depth has been NEGATIVE alpha in this regime — buy stabilization, not depth.
